summary.summary: Modifies the DynamicISF condition in Trio/Sources/APS/OpenAPSSwift/DetermineBasal/DynamicISF.swift so that the check which can disable dynISF now uses profile.halfBasalExerciseTarget instead of the previous legacy condition. The change removes a legacy dynISF-condition block and adjusts the conditional to return .off only when profile.highTemptargetRaisesSensitivity is true and the profile target is >= profile.halfBasalExerciseTarget. A small typo introduced in the diff was also fixed. (See PR and commits and the source diff.)
summary.userFacingImpact: Conservative: This change alters when dynamic ISF (dynISF) is disabled. According to the PR and commits, the previous condition could cause dynISF to be disabled for profiles with a target >= 118; the new condition compares the profile target to halfBasalExerciseTarget (noted in the PR as defaulting to 160), so dynISF will only be disabled when the user actually sets a TT at or above that halfBasalExerciseTarget threshold. This may re-enable dynISF in cases where it was previously suppressed by the legacy condition. Human review is recommended before deployment to confirm clinical/behavioral effects in specific setups.
